text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
Solid Tumor Next-Generation Sequencing вЂ” 523-Gene Panel

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Patient Name: Marcus R. Ellison
Date of Birth: 1969
Sex: Male
MRN: [REDACTED]
Ordering Physician: [Oncology Attending]
Indication: Metastatic melanoma, BRAF V600E IHC-positive; comprehensive genomic profiling for treatment selection and prognostication

Specimen ID: SP-2024-AXLN-RT-001
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core needle biopsy
Specimen Site: Right axillary lymph node metastasis
Date of Procedure: [see clinical record]
Percent Tumor Nuclei: 72%
Assay: Comprehensive Solid Tumor Panel вЂ” 523-gene hybrid-capture next-generation sequencing (DNA and RNA)
Sequencing Coverage: Mean exon coverage 650x; minimum coverage threshold 250x (met for all reportable regions)
Quality Control: PASS вЂ” adequate tumor cellularity, sufficient DNA yield (148 ng), library complexity satisfactory
Turnaround Time: 12 business days from specimen receipt

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
CLINICAL SUMMARY
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Marcus R. Ellison is a 55-year-old male with newly diagnosed Stage IV (AJCC 8th edition, M1c) metastatic melanoma, primary site skin of the upper back. The primary lesion is a nodular melanoma with Breslow thickness of 6.1 mm, Clark level V, ulceration present, mitotic rate 6/mm2, and lymphovascular invasion. Disease burden includes bilateral axillary lymphadenopathy with a right axillary conglomerate mass (4.8 cm), left cervical lymphadenopathy, six hepatic metastases (largest 3.1 cm), multiple cutaneous and subcutaneous satellite nodules, and a markedly elevated LDH of 642 U/L (approximately 2x ULN). BRAF V600E was detected by immunohistochemistry (VE1 antibody, strongly positive) on primary and nodal pathology. PD-L1 expression by 28-8 assay: TPS 25%, CPS 38. This specimen was obtained from the right axillary lymph node metastasis for comprehensive genomic profiling to guide systemic therapy selection.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
GENOMIC FINDINGS SUMMARY
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

SOMATIC MUTATIONS вЂ” TIER I/II (Actionable and Clinically Significant)

1. BRAF p.V600E (c.1799T>A)
   Gene Function: Serine/threonine kinase; MAPK pathway
   Variant Class: Missense mutation
   Allele Frequency: 48.2%
   Exon: 15
   Classification: ONCOGENIC вЂ” Tier I (FDA-approved therapy available)
   Clinical Significance: The BRAF p.V600E substitution (valine to glutamic acid at codon 600) is the most common driver mutation in cutaneous melanoma, present in approximately 50% of cases. This variant constitutively activates BRAF kinase signaling, driving RAS-independent activation of the MEK-ERK cascade and promoting tumor cell proliferation and survival. This specific alteration is the predictive biomarker for FDA-approved BRAF-targeted therapies including vemurafenib (Zelboraf), dabrafenib (Tafinlar), and encorafenib (Braftovi), as well as combination BRAF+MEK inhibitor regimens (dabrafenib + trametinib [Tafinlar + Mekinist]; vemurafenib + cobimetinib [Zelboraf + Cotellic]; encorafenib + binimetinib [Braftovi + Mektovi]). Allele frequency of 48.2% is consistent with a heterozygous mutation in a high-purity tumor specimen, with no evidence of allelic imbalance at this locus (see copy number data). BRAF amplification not detected at this timepoint.

2. PTEN p.R130* (c.388C>T)
   Gene Function: Tumor suppressor; PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway antagonist (lipid phosphatase)
   Variant Class: Nonsense mutation (premature stop codon)
   Allele Frequency: 41.1%
   Exon: 5
   Classification: PATHOGENIC вЂ” Loss of function, Tier II
   Clinical Significance: PTEN p.R130* introduces a premature termination codon, resulting in a truncated, non-functional protein and loss of phosphatase activity. PTEN loss is among the most frequently co-occurring alterations in BRAF-mutant melanoma (present in 10вЂ“30% of cases) and results in constitutive activation of the PI3K-AKT-mTOR signaling axis, which operates in parallel to and independently of the MAPK cascade. PTEN loss has been associated with reduced responsiveness to BRAF inhibitor monotherapy and to anti-PD-1 immunotherapy in some preclinical and clinical data. The co-occurrence of PTEN loss with BRAF V600E creates a two-hit oncogenic landscape that may contribute to aggressive clinical behavior. No evidence of PTEN homozygous deletion by copy number analysis at this locus (hemizygous loss with somatic mutation of retained allele suspected, consistent with VAF of 41.1% in 72% tumor).

3. TP53 p.R175H (c.524G>A)
   Gene Function: Tumor suppressor; cell cycle checkpoint, DNA damage response, apoptosis
   Variant Class: Missense mutation (gain-of-function)
   Allele Frequency: 39.8%
   Exon: 5
   Classification: PATHOGENIC вЂ” Gain-of-function, Tier II
   Clinical Significance: TP53 p.R175H is one of the canonical gain-of-function p53 "hotspot" mutations and among the most commonly identified TP53 alterations in human cancers. Unlike simple loss-of-function mutations, p.R175H confers oncogenic gain-of-function properties, including aberrant transcriptional activation of pro-oncogenic genes, disruption of wild-type p53 remaining in the cell (dominant-negative effect), impaired apoptotic response, promotion of cell migration and invasion, and interaction with chromatin remodeling complexes. The allele frequency of 39.8% is consistent with a clonal, heterozygous mutation in the dominant tumor clone. Concurrent CDKN2A loss (see below) eliminates an additional cell cycle safeguard, compounding the effect of p53 dysfunction.

SOMATIC MUTATIONS вЂ” TIER III (Variants of Unknown/Uncertain Significance with Relevance)

4. RAC1 p.P29S (c.85C>T)
   Gene Function: Rho GTPase; cytoskeletal organization, cell migration, MAPK signaling
   Variant Class: Missense mutation
   Allele Frequency: 11.4%
   Exon: 2
   Classification: LIKELY PATHOGENIC / Tier IIB вЂ” subclonal
   Clinical Significance: RAC1 p.P29S is the third most common mutation in cutaneous melanoma after BRAF and NRAS, present in approximately 4вЂ“7% of primary melanomas. This mutation is the canonical UV-signature change (C>T at a dipyrimidine site, consistent with SBS7 signature), creating a fast-cycling, constitutively active RAC1 GTPase that promotes melanocyte proliferation and transformation. The relatively lower allele frequency (11.4%) suggests a subclonal event, potentially arising later during clonal evolution. RAC1 p.P29S does not have approved targeted therapies but is recognized as a driver in melanoma. Its presence alongside a UV-dominant mutational signature supports UV exposure as the primary mutagenic etiology. No approved targeted therapies are available for RAC1 p.P29S at this time; investigational RAC1 inhibitors are in early-phase clinical trials.

5. TERT promoter c.-124C>T
   Gene Function: Telomerase reverse transcriptase; telomere maintenance
   Variant Class: Promoter mutation
   Allele Frequency: 52.3%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC вЂ” Tier IIB (prognostic significance)
   Clinical Significance: TERT promoter mutations (c.-124C>T and c.-146C>T) are the most common somatic mutations identified in cutaneous melanoma (found in 70вЂ“80% of metastatic cases) and are considered an early, clonal event in melanocyte transformation. The c.-124C>T variant creates a novel ETS transcription factor binding site, resulting in dramatically upregulated TERT expression and telomerase reactivation, conferring effective cellular immortality. In melanoma, TERT promoter mutation is strongly associated with aggressive disease biology, metastatic potential, and adverse prognosis, particularly in combination with other driver mutations such as BRAF V600E and CDKN2A loss. The high allele frequency (52.3%) is consistent with a clonal mutation present in the predominant tumor population. No directly targetable therapeutic implication.

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS

6. CDKN2A вЂ” Homozygous Deletion (chr9p21.3)
   Gene Function: Tumor suppressor; encodes p16/INK4A (CDK4/6 inhibitor) and p14/ARF (p53 pathway activator)
   Classification: PATHOGENIC вЂ” Tier II
   Clinical Significance: Homozygous deletion of CDKN2A (9p21.3) simultaneously abrogates both the p16-CDK4/6-RB cell cycle axis and the p14ARF-MDM2-p53 tumor suppressor pathway. CDKN2A homozygous deletion is present in approximately 30вЂ“40% of cutaneous melanomas and represents one of the most impactful tumor suppressor losses in melanoma pathogenesis. The combined effect of homozygous CDKN2A deletion and TP53 p.R175H gain-of-function mutation renders this tumor essentially devoid of functional p53 pathway control and CDK4/6-RB checkpoint activity. Note: loss of p14ARF further compromises MDM2 regulation, potentially amplifying the oncogenic effects of the co-occurring TP53 gain-of-function mutation.
   Estimated Copy Number: 0 (biallelic deletion confirmed; log2 ratio -3.2)

7. CCND1 Amplification (chr11q13.3)
   Gene Function: Cyclin D1; cell cycle progression, CDK4/6 activation, RB phosphorylation
   Estimated Copy Number: ~9 copies (log2 ratio +2.1)
   Classification: LIKELY PATHOGENIC вЂ” Tier IIB
   Clinical Significance: CCND1 amplification results in overexpression of Cyclin D1, which partners with CDK4 and CDK6 to phosphorylate and inactivate the RB tumor suppressor, driving cell cycle progression from G1 into S phase. In the context of concurrent CDKN2A homozygous deletion (which removes the endogenous CDK4/6 inhibitor p16), CCND1 amplification and CDKN2A loss functionally converge to completely deregulate the CDK4/6-RB axis. CCND1 amplification in melanoma has been associated with worse prognosis and more proliferative disease biology.

8. MYC Amplification (chr8q24.21)
   Gene Function: Transcription factor; cell growth, proliferation, metabolism, ribosome biogenesis, apoptosis suppression
   Estimated Copy Number: ~7 copies (log2 ratio +1.8)
   Classification: LIKELY PATHOGENIC вЂ” Tier IIB
   Clinical Significance: MYC amplification is a recurrent copy number gain in metastatic melanoma, observed in approximately 15вЂ“20% of advanced cases. MYC overexpression drives an extensive transcriptional program promoting tumor growth, suppresses apoptotic signaling, and cooperates with BRAF V600E to accelerate MAPK-driven proliferation. MYC amplification has been associated with more aggressive disease course and may contribute to resistance mechanisms. There are no approved MYC-targeted therapies; BET bromodomain inhibitors targeting BRD4-dependent MYC transcription are in clinical investigation.

NEGATIVE FINDINGS (Clinically Relevant Genes Assessed and Found Wild-Type)

- NRAS: Wild-type (codons 12, 13, 59, 61, 117, 146 all wild-type) вЂ” Note: NRAS and BRAF mutations are mutually exclusive in melanoma; absence of NRAS mutation expected and confirmed
- KRAS: Wild-type
- HRAS: Wild-type
- NF1: Wild-type вЂ” NF1 loss is found in approximately 14% of cutaneous melanomas and defines a distinct RAS-driven subtype; its absence here is consistent with a BRAF-driven tumor
- KIT: Wild-type вЂ” KIT mutation/amplification defines acral and mucosal melanoma subtypes; wild-type consistent with cutaneous primary
- GNAQ/GNA11: Wild-type вЂ” Mutations confined to uveal melanoma; wild-type consistent with cutaneous primary
- EGFR: Wild-type
- ALK: No rearrangement detected
- RET: No rearrangement detected
- MET: No amplification, no exon 14 skip mutation
- CDK4: Wild-type (not amplified) вЂ” Note: CDK4 amplification is an alternative mechanism of CDKN2A-pathway bypass; absent here; CDKN2A deletion is the operative mechanism
- MDM2: Wild-type
- BRCA1/BRCA2: No pathogenic variants detected (germline testing recommended separately per genetic counseling referral)

FUSION/REARRANGEMENT ANALYSIS
- No oncogenic gene fusions or rearrangements detected by RNA-seq fusion analysis
- Specifically: no BRAF fusions, no ALK/RET/ROS1/NTRK1/NTRK2/NTRK3 fusions identified

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB)
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
TMB: 18.4 mutations per megabase (mut/Mb)
Interpretation: INTERMEDIATE-HIGH
Context: TMB-high threshold (в‰Ґ10 mut/Mb per FDA pembrolizumab indication) is met. Cutaneous melanoma as a class has among the highest TMBs of any solid tumor, driven by UV-induced mutagenesis (C>T transitions at dipyrimidine sites). This TMB of 18.4 mut/Mb places the tumor in the intermediate-high range for melanoma (median TMB for metastatic melanoma is approximately 15вЂ“20 mut/Mb). High TMB is associated with increased neoantigen burden and improved responses to immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy in multiple tumor types. Combined with a CPS of 38 and TPS of 25% for PD-L1, this tumor has features that may be associated with immunotherapy responsiveness.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI) STATUS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
MSI Status: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
Number of MSI loci assessed: 114
Number of unstable loci: 1/114 (0.9%)
Interpretation: MSS. Mismatch repair deficiency-related immunotherapy eligibility (pembrolizumab MSI-H indication) does not apply based on this assay. High TMB-driven immunotherapy responsiveness remains independently relevant (pembrolizumab TMB-H indication).

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
MUTATIONAL SIGNATURE ANALYSIS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
Dominant Signature: SBS7a (UV signature, C>T substitutions at TpC sites, 58.4% of mutations)
Secondary Signature: SBS7b (UV-associated, CC>TT tandem mutations, 24.1% of mutations)
Minor Signature: SBS1 (age-related, CpG deamination, 9.2% of mutations)
Other: SBS5 (background, 8.3% of mutations)

Interpretation: The mutational landscape is overwhelmingly dominated by ultraviolet radiation-associated signatures (SBS7a and SBS7b together account for 82.5% of mutations), consistent with cumulative UV-induced DNA damage as the primary etiologic mechanism. This signature pattern is characteristic of sun-exposed cutaneous melanoma arising in the context of outdoor occupational and recreational UV exposure, as reported for this patient. The high proportion of UV-signature mutations is consistent with the elevated TMB and supports the primary diagnosis of cutaneous melanoma. No APOBEC (SBS2/SBS13), homologous recombination deficiency (SBS3), tobacco (SBS4), or other exogenous carcinogen signatures are detected.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
GERMLINE DISCLAIMER
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
This is a somatic tumor-only assay. The allele frequencies of detected variants (BRAF p.V600E AF 48.2%, PTEN p.R130* AF 41.1%, TP53 p.R175H AF 39.8%, TERT promoter c.-124C>T AF 52.3%) are consistent with clonal somatic mutations in a high-cellularity tumor specimen and are not consistent with germline alterations. Constitutional BRAF mutation is not suspected. However, formal germline testing is recommended for BRCA1/2, CDKN2A (given family history of melanoma in maternal aunt), and BAP1 to assess hereditary cancer predisposition, and genetic counseling referral is strongly advised.

Note: CDKN2A germline pathogenic variants are found in approximately 20вЂ“40% of familial melanoma kindreds; the family history of melanoma in this patient's maternal aunt warrants formal germline assessment.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
INTEGRATED MOLECULAR INTERPRETATION
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
This metastatic cutaneous melanoma harbors a complex genomic landscape with multiple co-occurring oncogenic and tumor suppressor alterations. The dominant driver is BRAF p.V600E, an actionable Tier I alteration with direct therapeutic implications. Concurrent alterations include:

- Loss of CDKN2A (homozygous deletion): abrogates G1/S checkpoint via p16-CDK4/6-RB and p14ARF-MDM2-p53 pathways
- PTEN p.R130* (loss of function): constitutive PI3K-AKT pathway activation, operating as a parallel survival signal to MAPK
- TP53 p.R175H (gain-of-function): dominant oncogenic p53 activity compounding loss of p14ARF-p53 regulation
- TERT promoter c.-124C>T: clonal, associated with aggressive biology and poor prognosis in melanoma
- RAC1 p.P29S: subclonal UV-signature GTPase activator; no currently actionable target
- CCND1 amplification and MYC amplification: convergent copy number alterations driving cell cycle dysregulation and proliferation

The combination of BRAF V600E, CDKN2A deletion, PTEN loss, CCND1 amplification, and MYC amplification defines a genomically aggressive tumor with convergent MAPK hyperactivation and cell cycle checkpoint abrogation. PTEN loss and PI3K pathway activation may represent a baseline co-existing resistance mechanism that could limit depth or duration of BRAF/MEK inhibitor response over time; however, BRAF-targeted therapy is the standard-of-care first-line option for BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic melanoma with high disease burden. The TMB of 18.4 mut/Mb and PD-L1 TPS 25% / CPS 38 suggest that immunotherapy (anti-PD-1 or combination checkpoint blockade) may be effective if/when BRAF-targeted therapy is exhausted or as an alternative treatment sequence.

The UV-dominant mutational signature is consistent with occupational UV exposure history and supports the clinical and pathologic diagnosis of primary cutaneous melanoma.

Tier I вЂ” FDA-Approved Therapy with Matched Biomarker in this Tumor Type:
вЂў BRAF p.V600E в†’ Dabrafenib + Trametinib (Tafinlar + Mekinist) [FDA-approved for BRAF V600E/K metastatic melanoma]
вЂў BRAF p.V600E в†’ Vemurafenib + Cobimetinib (Zelboraf + Cotellic) [FDA-approved]
вЂў BRAF p.V600E в†’ Encorafenib + Binimetinib (Braftovi + Mektovi) [FDA-approved]
вЂў BRAF p.V600E в†’ Vemurafenib (Zelboraf) monotherapy [FDA-approved; combination preferred]
вЂў BRAF p.V600E в†’ Dabrafenib (Tafinlar) monotherapy [FDA-approved; combination preferred]
вЂў TMB в‰Ґ10 mut/Mb в†’ Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) [FDA-approved, tumor-agnostic TMB-H indication]

Tier II вЂ” Potentially Relevant Based on Biomarker / Off-Label or Investigational:
вЂў PTEN loss / PI3K pathway activation в†’ PI3K-AKT-mTOR inhibitor combinations (investigational in melanoma; clinical trial consideration)
вЂў CCND1 amplification, CDKN2A loss в†’ CDK4/6 inhibitors (investigational in melanoma; CDKN2A loss renders this tumor theoretically very sensitive to direct CDK4/6 inhibition; clinical trial consideration)
вЂў MYC amplification в†’ BET bromodomain inhibitors (early investigational)
вЂў High TMB + PD-L1 expression в†’ Nivolumab В± ipilimumab (Opdivo В± Yervoy); pembrolizumab (Keytruda) [anti-PD-1 strategies broadly applicable, consistent with standard-of-care options]

LIMITATIONS
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
вЂў This assay detects alterations in the 523 genes on the panel; alterations in genes not included on the panel will not be detected.
вЂў FFPE-derived DNA may introduce artifactual C>T transitions; stringent bioinformatic filtering for FFPE artifact has been applied. All reported variants exceed artifact thresholds.
вЂў This assay does not assess epigenetic alterations (methylation, histone modification) or non-coding regulatory regions outside the reported TERT promoter locus.
вЂў Tumor heterogeneity may result in subclonal alterations being under-represented (minimum reportable AF: 5%).
вЂў RNA fusion analysis is dependent on RNA quality and tumor content; RNA QC: PASS (DV200 = 61%).
вЂў Germline variants cannot be excluded solely based on allele frequency for variants in which loss of heterozygosity may occur. Germline testing recommended for clinical variants where hereditary predisposition is a consideration.
вЂў Results should be interpreted in the context of complete clinical, pathologic, and radiographic information by a qualified oncologist.
